text: This is the case of a sixty-nine-year-old female with a medical history significant for diabetes and hypertension. She presented with a gastrointestinal bleed. On evaluation with upper endoscopy, a hyperemic submucosal mass in the duodenal bulb was found. Biopsy was positive for neoplastic cells, but there was no specific tissue diagnosis. A CT scan confirmed a two centimeter by two centimeter by two point five centimeter submucosal mass This was within the first portion of the duodenum. It was heterogeneously enhancing without evidence of metastatic disease.

The surgeon establishes pneumoperitoneum using a Veress needle and places laparoscopic ports. A liver retractor is deployed, and the duodenum is circumferentially mobilized using blunt dissection with laparoscopic instruments and a ligature energy device. The dissection reveals the posterior wall and tumor boundaries, avoiding the portal triad structures. The mass is confirmed not to extend into surrounding structures.

The surgeon uses a hook cautery to create an enterotomy in the proximal duodenum. A ligature energy device is employed to circumferentially resect the mass, ensuring hemostasis and maintaining a one-centimeter margin around it. The excised tumor is placed in an endocatch bag and set aside.

The surgeon performs a transverse repair of the duodenum using 2-0 Ethibond sutures in a simple interrupted fashion, creating a single-layer closure. An upper endoscopy is used to evaluate the repair, with air insufflation of the stomach and duodenum and saline filling of the peritoneum to check for leaks, which are absent. The operation concludes with the placement of a number ten Jackson Pratt drain at the repair site.

On postoperative day number one, our patient underwent an upper GI series with gastrographin. This showed no evidence of stricture or leak. Our patient was then placed on a clear liquid diet on which she remained on discharge on post-operative day number three.
Pathology revealed a three point five centimeter low grade gist with negative margins.
